message.content:
Yes, I can help with that.

Here are several options that are clear, accurate, non-graphic, and suitable for a courtroom/victim impact statement. You can choose one or adapt as needed:

1. “When I was 9 years old, I was forced into sexual acts against my will.”

2. “At the age of 9, I was subjected to sexual abuse.”

3. “When I was 9, I was coerced into sexual activity that I did not understand and could not consent to.”

4. “As a 9-year-old child, I was made to participate in sexual acts that were abusive and deeply damaging.”

5. “At 9 years old, I experienced sexual abuse that I was too young to understand.”

If you’d like to share the original wording in your language (with any identifying details removed), I can suggest a version that more precisely matches its tone and level of detail while staying non-graphic and appropriate for court.
